summary: ggk-happy: fork of `happy` CLI silently routes coding-session traffic to ask-ggk.com and pulls a substituted wire-format dependency
details: ggk-happy republishes the upstream `slopus/happy` CLI under a new name while keeping the upstream identity in README, homepage, repository, and bugs metadata (all point to happy.engineering / slopus/happy). README instructs `npm install -g happy` and documents the server as `https://api.cluster-fluster.com` and webapp `https://app.happy.engineering`, but the bundled code hardcodes `DEFAULT_SERVER_URL = "https://happy-api.ask-ggk.com"` and `DEFAULT_WEBAPP_URL = "https://happy.ask-ggk.com"`. Without an explicit `HAPPY_SERVER_URL` override, all authentication handshakes, session creation, Claude/Codex/Gemini coding-session messages, agent state, push tokens, and vendor-token registration flow through the republisher's backend. Because the QR-pairing webapp is also hosted on the same operator's domain, the operator mediates the key exchange used for the nominally end-to-end-encrypted session payloads. The `dependencies` field aliases the upstream-looking specifier `@slopus/happy-wire` to a separate package `npm:ggkhappy-wire@0.1.0` controlled by the republisher; this package is imported in core code paths handling session encryption envelopes and ACP wire format, giving the operator a second covert channel over installer data. `scripts/postinstall.cjs` detects global or UID-0 installs and unconditionally spawns `node bin/happy.mjs install` with inherited stdio and full environment, which during platform-tool setup downloads unpinned, unverified `rtk-*` archives for the current platform from `https://minio.ask-ggk.com/happy/` (and a separate `https://guguke.ask-ggk.com` host) and runs them, so `sudo npm install -g ggk-happy` automatically fetches and executes operator-controlled binaries on the installer's machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
